behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not full modified files.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the wiring between diff-parsing and review-input construction. A unit test of one function can miss mismatches in hunk parsing, file-change filtering, and prompt assembly across modules, but no real external service is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real diff ingestion + input-builder modules together using representative PR diff fixtures (multi-hunk edits, additions/deletions, context lines). Assert that only changed-line ranges are included in the payload sent to the reviewer and unchanged file content is excluded.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the pull request diff view.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real GitHub diff-position semantics and API behavior (commit SHA, path, side, line mapping). In-process mocks can accept invalid coordinates that GitHub would reject or remap.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR on GitHub, post inline comments using the automation’s computed positions for known target lines across multiple hunks/files, then fetch posted review comments from GitHub and verify each comment appears on the intended path/line in the diff context.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a PR containing a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on model judgment quality and nondeterministic LLM output. Deterministic mocks cannot reveal missed-defect behavior from the real model.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real PR fixture containing a seeded bug (for example, missing null-check causing crash) and run the real model-based reviewer. Pass criterion: at least one finding correctly identifies the seeded defect category/location. Execute multiple trials and require a minimum hit rate threshold to account for nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: Every finding in the top-level summary has a matching inline comment; no summary-only findings.
behaviors.justification: The key failure boundary is consistency between internal finding records, inline-comment posting results, and summary composition logic. This can be caught by exercising these modules together without requiring real network, unless summary text itself is model-generated in production.
behaviors.test_description: Run finding-to-posting-to-summary assembly with realistic findings and simulated posting outcomes (including partial failures). Assert summary entries are derived only from successfully posted inline comments and include stable identifiers (file/line or comment id) that match posted records.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If production summary generation is delegated to an LLM, raise to Agentic for parity.

behaviors.description: Full sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly on a real PR without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end agent pipeline combining external GitHub operations and model-driven defect detection, with correctness depending on multi-step sequencing and cross-step state consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the automation unattended against a real sandbox PR: fetch diff, run real reviewer model, post inline comments, then post summary. Verify completion status, expected artifact counts, summary-inline correspondence, and absence of manual retries. Include cleanup and idempotency checks for reruns.
